title: glechoma bombaiensis
intro: Glechoma bombaiensis, also known as Himalayan mint, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the mint family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been found to help soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of respiratory problems. The health benefits of Glechoma bombaiensis include its ability to aid in the treatment of colds, coughs, and fever, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or infused oil to promote wound healing.
